tiers.description: By touching a corpse, the mummy animates it as a simple Lifeless servant that can answer yes/no questions about things it knew in life.
By touch and force of will, the mummy raises a human corpse as an Awakened Corpse Thrall — a mindless killing machine that hunts all living things unless given orders.
The mummy briefly resurrects a recently deceased person to full life, restoring body and memories, though the miracle eventually fades and the dead crumble to permanent dust.
The mummy invokes Fate's blessing upon himself, gaining a hanging bonus that lets him reflexively reroll failed dice on Attribute and Skill rolls by spending Willpower.
The mummy extends this blessing of good fortune to himself or any person he has directly touched, granting them the same reroll benefit for the next hour.

The mummy transforms into a divine avatar of his decree — gaining animal features of his decree, growing in size, becoming an unmistakable supernatural presence with heightened senses and cosmic authority.
The mummy conjures a small orb of blue-tinted light that follows his commands, illuminates nearby ghosts as if visible, and enables the dead to communicate freely.
The mummy issues a single binding command to any ghost or revenant he can perceive, compelling it to obey in good faith within the limits of its nature.
The mummy reshapes a ghost's very soul — each success allows him to alter its Vice, Virtue, Morality, memories, anchors, or fundamental spiritual nature.
The mummy walks unmolested by the natural world — all non-sapient animals become blind to his presence for one full day, granting perfect stealth against the animal kingdom.
The mummy crowns himself a god of beasts for a day; Animal Ken rolls are easier, and he can automatically command non-magical animals to carry out complex instructions.
The mummy curses a target or area, turning all non-magical animals in the vicinity into savage attackers hell-bent on destroying the target utterly and without mercy.
The mummy curses a target's Skills, causing any dice pool using cursed Skills to suffer an increased target number for one day.
The curse intensifies, penalizing all of the target's attacks rather than specific Skills, reducing the target number of all hostile actions against the mummy's enemies for one hour.
Unleashed reflexively to mar another's actions, this curse adds +1 to the target number of any single roll and causes any failure to be treated as a dramatic failure.
The mummy scatters prophetic dream-visions across the cosmos, guided by Fate to allies and enemies — sending warnings, instructions, or inspiration through sleep.
The mummy sends targeted dream-visions to a specific person when they sleep — either seductive allure that creates vulnerability or terrifying nightmares that inflict a flux derangement.
The mummy curses a perceivable target's aura with ruin, love, or grandeur — imposing a lasting social effect that shapes how others perceive and interact with the accursed.
The mummy attunes to the earth itself, gaining immunity to fall damage from earthen surfaces and resistance to environmental harm, and can telekinetically command loose earthen material within range.
Earthen material from the environment assembles around the mummy into a massive stone exoskeleton, greatly increasing her Size, armor, Strength, and physical capabilities.

The mummy unleashes a catastrophic earthquake centered on herself — roughly magnitude 6.0, devastating within ten miles — and disturbs all local ghosts, granting them free rein to haunt the quake zone.
The mummy summons a blazing ankh glyph and focuses its healing light on a mortal within range, temporarily reinforcing the beneficiary's Strength or Stamina as if supported by a Sekhem 1 mummy.
The ankh's empowering nimbus grants a single selection of temporary Merits from a set list to a beneficiary, lasting twelve hours or one day for mortals with the Vice of Envy.
The summoned ankh grants life itself to any animate being in its golden light, healing lethal to bashing and potentially attempting the impossible — resurrection of a recently deceased mortal.
The mummy's touch inflicts a supernatural wasting disease on any animate being — living, dead, or ghostly — that prevents healing and deals ongoing damage until resisted or death follows.
The mummy ritually curses an object held by her cult, embedding a death trap within it that triggers fatal consequences against anyone who steals or damages it without authorization.
Black clouds of supernatural darkness spread from the mummy in a wide radius, attacking enemies each turn, blinding all within, and dragging hostile characters into Twilight.
The mummy's senses warp to perceive an object or material's full physical and metaphysical composition, revealing all reactive properties including supernatural traits and vulnerabilities.
The mummy transmutes matter to shift its phase — solid to liquid, liquid to gas, or the reverse — temporarily reshaping any non-relic inanimate object within perception range.

The mummy masters matter itself by touch, permanently transmuting any inanimate non-relic object into another substance of equal or lesser size, potentially refining base matter into precious alloys or gems.
The mummy attunes her consciousness to an artificial structure she occupies, perceiving its full layout and all barriers within through a displaced tactile sense that travels at twice her Speed.
The mummy exerts telekinetic force throughout the structure, controlling doors, furniture, utilities, and security systems as extensions of her own will.
The mummy connects two doorways within the same structure, creating a magical shortcut portal that allows anyone who fits through the entrance to instantly travel between the two locations.
The mummy conjures a pale golden mystical fire upon a chosen target within range — a flame that doesn't consume what it burns and through which the mummy can speak as if present.
The mummy suppresses supernatural fire in a large area, swallowing it into an implosion of mutual annihilation that extinguishes magical flames and disrupts fire-based powers.
The mummy screams with divine solar voice, igniting in a blinding flash brighter than the noon sun that inflicts aggravated damage on all ghosts, Lifeless, and vampires in the radius.
The mummy wraps herself in an invisible shell of exuded Sekhem for one hour — hostile magic targeting her must overcome the higher of her Sekhem or Ka rating to function.
The mummy reflexively deploys her Sekhem ward against any incoming supernatural power, automatically suppressing it without a roll, though the defense collapses instantly upon activation.
The mummy reveals all temporary enchantments affecting a target within range, then scours away magic that isn't native to the target's existence — stripping active spells and temporary Disciplines.
The mummy conjures a swirling cloud of incense smoke or sandstorm grit that follows him, concealing all within it and dealing automatic bashing damage to those who breathe it.

The mummy sprouts wings of smoke and fire, transforming into a towering alien presence with flight, a commanding voice audible 500 yards away, expanded sight measured in miles, and supernatural beauty.
The mummy becomes a pillar of divine fire reaching into the distant sky — visible for 50 miles — granting Stamina bonuses to all who deliberately follow him and marking him as a holy beacon.
The mummy vomits forth a living Lifeless scarab — a supernatural talisman-creature that protects whoever wears it, subtracting dice from rolls that would cause physical harm.
This scarab bites a tiny piece of the mummy's flesh to gain lasting existence, becoming an independent resurrection insurance vessel that carries the mummy's Sekhem back to his cult.
The mummy disgorges a swarm of thousands of enchanted scarabs that act as a weapon or, when paired with the first tier, affix to allies as protective talismans valid for one hour.
The mummy gazes at the sky and intuitively knows his exact geographical location relative to any reference point with satellite-like precision, and can always retrace his steps through a maze.
The mummy speaks a word of power reshaping cosmic genesis and calls down a blazing meteorite on a chosen target within one mile who is exposed to the open sky.
The mummy reads the constellations and receives one important cryptic clue from Fate about his most pressing current dilemma, shrouded in metaphor but not wholly useless.
The mummy becomes highly resistant to harm from plants and environmental factors, ignores injury from plant-based projectiles, and can draw poison from a living person through skin-to-skin touch.
The mummy causes edible plants suited to the local terrain to spontaneously grow within range, creating a productive garden from bare earth that yields enough food to sustain many people.
The mummy curses a target with unnatural fertility — their next mating with an opposite-sex partner results in automatic conception, and both parents gain lasting healing and restored youth.
The mummy cloaks himself in an aura of anonymity lasting for hours — mortals who encounter him forget or misremember the experience entirely once he departs their perception.

The mummy makes a touch attack that sends the target a compressed vision of chosen memories, or interrogates the target's own mind by posing any question and extracting relevant recollections in the dreamscape.
The mummy touches a target and permanently steals specific memories — posing any question and absorbing the relevant recollections into his own mind, erasing them from the victim forever.
The mummy strikes the ground, cracking open a fissure through which a spring of pure fresh water erupts, flowing for months and supporting a moderate oasis before finally drying up.
The mummy curses all water within two miles, transmuting standing and flowing water into human blood for hours — a persistent transformation that kills aquatic life and harms vampires who drink it.
The mummy parts a large body of water, creating a corridor of dry land through the depths that follows her as she walks and closes like a crushing wall behind any who fail to keep pace.
The mummy invokes the language beneath all languages, causing any written text within touch to physically rearrange into the reader's native tongue, though ciphers and metaphors may resist.

The mummy fills his mind with the tongue of Irem; for the rest of the scene all who hear him understand his meaning perfectly regardless of language barriers, and Intimidation rolls against mortals become easier.
The mummy unleashes a terrible anti-language, cursing all nearby characters whose Resolve + Sekhem fails to match his roll with the loss of all ability to comprehend or produce speech and writing.
The mummy summons a ghost by creating a new anchor for it, drawing any spirit meeting chosen filters — rough age, temperament, potential danger — to materialize at the current location.
The mummy consecrates a large area as his domain, enriching it with ghostly energy that grants resident ghosts additional Numina, Maddening, and enhanced capabilities.
The mummy summons an ancient chthonic entity from beyond reality — an enormous Lifeless predator that devours ghosts to grow and only marginally obeys its summoner.
The mummy calls the nearest intact corpse within range to animate as a nenitu — a shambling Lifeless creature whose infectious bite creates more of its kind until a self-sustaining pack forms.

The mummy prepares a corpse through a midnight-to-sunset ritual, allowing its ghost to voluntarily touch the flesh at dawn and transition from spirit into a revenant with Passions the mummy helps shape.
The mummy targets a manifesting ghost and fuses it into a human corpse as an intruder — a possessing spirit with a destructive agenda and the power to return to flesh multiple times.
The mummy gains second sight for one hour — she can see, physically strike, and drain Essence from ghosts and revenants as though they were fully corporeal.
The mummy bestows a chosen supernatural Numen upon any ghost or revenant within range, empowering the dead with borrowed ability to serve her current story needs.
The mummy opens her mouth impossibly wide to reveal a vortex that pulls all visible ghosts toward her, devouring them into an inner lake of fire where they burn but cannot cease to exist.

The mummy launches blasts of scouring sand from her hands or mouth, dealing lethal ranged damage with autofire capability — and as a death curse, may fire a single blast at her killer ignoring wound penalties.
The desert sands swallow a target standing on sand, engulfing them in a grapple that can entomb them underground, or the mummy may disperse into sand and reform at another sandy location nearby.
The mummy calls a massive sandstorm covering 100 yards in every direction that persists for an hour — obscuring vision, penalizing all actions within, and burying those who stay prone.
